title: The Vital Algorithm: A Medical Thriller (Second Edition)
author: Walter Kyo Csoelle
description: Kindle Edition by Walter Kyo Csoelle. If an algorithm had to choose between saving a wealthy CEO or a beloved kindergarten teacher, how would it decide? Dr. Jana Bravcova returns to Bratislava to join MedCore, where VITAL-7 and the Social Utility Score price patients by economic value. As she uncovers a so-called glitch that was actually murder, she must choose between compliance and stopping the system. Written by Dr. Lubomir Polascin, this second edition adds a Developer's Cut appendix, an updated AI chapter for 2026, a new afterword ('Leaving the Factory'), and a reading group guide.

title: Vital Algorithm: A Novel of Code and Conscience (Print)
author: Walter Kyo Csoelle

title: BLOOD EQUITY: Some Cures Are Deadlier Than Disease
author: Walter Kyo Csoelle
description: Kindle Edition by Walter Kyo Csoelle. In the post-socialist Republic of Carpathia, Severograd faces predatory healthcare corporatization as VitaCorp expands and targets independent nephrologist Dr. Andrej Varga. Refusing to sell his clinic, Varga uncovers lethal cost-cutting practices including involuntary discharge of unprofitable patients, reuse of single-use dialyzers, and shortened dialysis sessions. With whistleblower nurse Lena, he gathers evidence while enduring bureaucratic sabotage, media attacks, and physical intimidation. The conflict culminates at the EU Healthcare Summit, where Varga exposes falsified records, contaminated filters, and systemic corruption tied to corporate and political actors. Although key figures fall, the novel ends with a realistic warning that the deeper system remains and ethical medicine must be defended continuously.

title: Pulse of the Body: A Novel of Medicine, Humanity, and the Edge of Life
author: Walter Kyo Csoelle

title: Acute renal failure and acute kidney injury
author: Lubomir Polascin et al.
description: Academic paper highlighting these conditions as serious medical problems requiring collaboration among specialists and often intensive hospital care, including extracorporeal elimination therapy. Available on ResearchGate: https://www.researchgate.net/publication/323586471_Acute_renal_failure_and_acute_kidney_injury

title: Novšie aspekty antihypertenzívnej liečby u pacientov s diabetickou nefropatiou
author: Silvester Krčméry; Ľubomír Polaščín; Rastislav Tahotný; Zuzana Gábrišová; Klára Soláriková
description: ref.císlo: UK.Bratislava.vtls000259231. In: Forum diabetologicum. ISSN 1805-3807. Roč. 1, č. 1 (2012), s. 24-31.

title: Nefrológia: Ochorenia obličiek
author: Ľubomír Polaščín et al.
description: Chapter in textbook Interná medicína (Hulín, Kiňová, team of authors).

title: DLHODOBÁ ÚSPEŠNÁ REMISIA IDIOPATICKEJ MEMBRÁNOVEJ NEFROPATIE APLIKÁCIOU PONTICELLIHO SCHÉMY IMUNOSUPRESÍVNEJ LIEČBY
author: Ľubomír Polaščín, Monika Alaxinová, Gabriela Karasová, Jana Kalatová
description: 38. kongres Slovenskej nefrologickej spoločnosti (Program a abstrakty). Abstract concerning long-term successful remission of idiopathic membranous nephropathy using the Ponticelli immunosuppressive treatment scheme.

title: Kontinuálna renálna nahrádzajúca terapia (KRNT, CRRT) pri AOP (AKI)
author: MUDr. Ľubomír Polaščín
description: Krajský nefrologický seminár – World Kidney Day, Bratislava. Specific clinical exploration on continuous renal replacement therapy in acute kidney injury.

title: Nefrológ Polaščín: Na zlyhanie obličiek by pacient v dnešnej dobe nemal zomrieť
author: Pravda.sk (Interview s Ľubomírom Polaščínom)
description: Major medical interview published in Pravda on February 9, 2015. Discusses Dr. Polaščín's medical journey, the intersection of medicine and engineering, and the state of modern dialysis.

title: Vliv použití dialyzačního roztoku s citrátovou složkou v kyselém koncentrátu na množství použitého heparinu při dialyzačním ošetření
author: L. Polaščín
description: Academic abstract presented at the conference "Vývoj léčby a ošetřovatelské péče u pacientů s onemocněním ledvin" (CNNA). Analyzing the impact of citrate component dialysate solutions on heparin dosing.
